players.name: Xeebs
matchupItemFlags: no magic resist vs a 3-magical enemy comp

notes: Their main damage was Grux (34k physical); 1/5 of us itemized armor — that hurt.
Enemy ran 2/5 on or near their meta core.

draftNote: 1 of five queued off a bottom-two lane: Cuban Noobster (midlane→carry).

coaching.headline: We won a big mid-game fight, then handed it all back chasing into their jungle for nothing.
coaching.team: Three lanes were favored, and at 20.2 we won a real team fight (5-3) — that should have been the snowball. Instead we immediately over-extended: the 21.6 fight at their Fangtooth (1-3, four of us into a place we couldn't hold) and the 22.2 follow (0-2, opened two bodies down) gave it straight back, and Grux's team took Fangtooth, Orb Prime and Primal Fangtooth on the bounce. The game ended on another bad trade — a 29-min 3-man deep in their jungle (1-3). Grux was the threat and only one of us built armor. The towers say it plainly: 2 to their 10. After winning at 20.2, the call was to take an objective on OUR terms, not to dive their Fangtooth.
coaching.whatShiftedIt: Throwing the 20.2 fight we won (5-3) by immediately diving their Fangtooth at 21.6 (1-3) and 22.2 (0-2) — which handed them four straight majors and the map.

perPlayer.7c8ab99c-31c5-4ea5-a6fa-e0d6417c3f1b: The Fey was our best lane — 7 kills, favored, and Mr_Meat31 won the early skirmishes at 9 and 9.9. Then came the pull into the 21.6 dive on their Fangtooth (1-3) right after the team had won at 20.2. Mr_Meat31 had the lead; the move was to keep pressuring where The Fey was strong, not to follow into their jungle.
perPlayer.16f28434-561f-4f23-9454-40321f4a1ef3: Phase died 4 times and carried no magic resist into a 3-magical comp (the flag), which is why Phase got deleted in the fights. Goldilocks324 was in the 21.6 Fangtooth throw and the 22.2 follow. As support, Goldilocks324 is the one who should be pinging off a dive into their territory after a won fight — that's where the game slipped.
perPlayer.a0c393cc-1437-4bdc-8168-64676ce2166c: Lt. Belica was the unfavored lane (Gideon owned it all game) and 7 deaths showed it. Cuban Noobster was in every losing fight — 21.6, 22.2, and the 29-min throw deep in their jungle (1-3). With the lane behind, the job is zone-control from the back, not being in the front of dives into their half.
perPlayer.10edbf65-457d-48d0-9151-9df5b4fcb40a: Skylar had the early edge and a clean stat line (4/3/6, normal game), but Willy Guggenheim wasn't there for the close — Skylar doesn't appear in the final losing fights. The Vanquisher landed at 25 and Plasma Blade at 30; with the team throwing leads, that damage needed to be present in those team fights to punish, not split off.
perPlayer.a412baf3-dd17-4f89-8ecf-9d0704f3a79e: Feng Mao was favored but Xeebs died 8 times and ran no magic resist into a 3-magical comp. Xeebs was in the 21.6 and 22.2 dives and the 29-min jungle throw. The jungler sets the pace — after winning at 20.2, the read was to ward and take a safe objective, not to keep diving Grux's team on their side.

notes: They traded the fight for Mini Prime across the map — we won bodies but gave the objective.
They traded the fight for Mini Prime across the map — we won bodies but gave the objective.
They traded the fight for Fangtooth across the map — we won bodies but gave the objective.
Xeebs (jungle) was alive and never joined — get them to the fight and the count changes.
We opened it a 2-body down (2v4) — the numbers were lost before the fight was.
Mr_Meat31 (offlane) was dead — went down 36s earlier, so this was never a full-strength fight.
Goldilocks324 (support) was dead — went down 32s earlier, so this was never a full-strength fight.
Mr_Meat31 (offlane) was alive and ahead in lane — a shove-and-rotate there flips a 1-3 into a numbers advantage.
Willy Guggenheim (carry) was alive and never joined — get them to the fight and the count changes.

threats: Chain-CC risk (5 hard-CC abilities, ~3.5s of lockdown): Riktor Riplash (pull), Riktor Shock Therapy (silence 0.8s), Riktor Skewer (suppress 1.25s), Kwang Surge of the Heavens (knockup 0.5s), Grux Crush (knockup 0.5s). Don't group tight into it — stagger the engage and hold mobility/cleanse for the key one.
synergy: Our engage/CC: Lt. Belica Neural Disruptor (silence 0.5s). Our kills come from landing burst INSIDE that CC window — whoever lands it, the rest must follow up immediately.
We have sustain (Phase) — favor the longer fight where our heals out-grind them.
Strong AoE/teamfight kit — group at choke points and fight on top of objectives.

ourKits.combo: Bramble Patch slow → Harvest Nettles stacks → Untamed Growth DoT → Fly Trap pull ult
ourKits.winCondition: Fly Trap a clumped team and detonate AoE on the pull
ourKits.passive: takedowns spawn growths + grant move speed
ourKits.playWith: engage that clumps for her Fly Trap
ourKits.playAgainst: spread out vs the pull; she's squishy — dive her

ourKits.combo: Telekinetic Link an ally → Psychic Flare blind → Energy Lance → Hyperflux buff ult
ourKits.winCondition: buff a hyper-carry through the Link and Hyperflux for a teamfight
ourKits.passive: abilities heal her and her linked ally
ourKits.playWith: the strongest carry to Link and supercharge
ourKits.playAgainst: kill the linked carry, not Phase; break the link timing

ourKits.combo: Seismic Assault knockup → Void Bomb → Neural Disruptor silence ult, Void Drone mana drain
ourKits.winCondition: knock up + silence a target and burst with missing-HP scaling
ourKits.playAgainst: dodge Seismic Assault; she's immobile — dive when ult is down

ourKits.combo: Plasma Grenade → Hypercharge on-hit → Air Assault reposition → Atomiser ult
ourKits.winCondition: Hypercharge and kite-shoot from Air Assault safety
ourKits.passive: extra jump; kills cause corpse explosions
ourKits.playWith: a frontline so she can free-fire airborne
ourKits.playAgainst: CC her out of Air Assault; she's squishy on the ground

ourKits.combo: Reaping Dash → Hamstring slows → Safeguard spellshield → Earth Shatter execute
ourKits.winCondition: trade with the spellshield up and execute low targets with Earth Shatter
ourKits.passive: permanent power on unique hero takedowns
ourKits.playWith: setup that holds a target for the execute
ourKits.playAgainst: bait Safeguard's spellshield before committing the key CC

enemyKits.combo: Riplash pull → Shock Therapy silence → Skewer suppress ult
enemyKits.winCondition: pull a target in and chain silence+suppress for a guaranteed kill
enemyKits.playWith: burst that deletes his pulled/suppressed target
enemyKits.playAgainst: respect Riplash range — stay spaced; cleanse/QSS the suppress

enemyKits.combo: Void Breach slow → Cosmic Rift → Black Hole on the grouped enemies
enemyKits.winCondition: land Black Hole / Cosmic Rift on a clumped team for AoE burst
enemyKits.passive: an ability-empowered basic tethers a target for bonus damage
enemyKits.playWith: hard engage that clumps enemies into his Black Hole
enemyKits.playAgainst: immobile with long cooldowns — dodge the skillshots and dive him once Black Hole is down

enemyKits.combo: Judgement tether → Surge knockup → Fury of the Heavens slow, recall the blade
enemyKits.winCondition: tether a target, knock up and brawl with energized basics
enemyKits.playWith: follow-up while he tethers and knocks up
enemyKits.playAgainst: bait his blade-recall mobility, then punish when it's placed

enemyKits.combo: Piercing Shot poke → Heightened Senses AS → Hail of Arrows zone → Inner Fire ult
enemyKits.winCondition: ramp Relentless + AS and sustained-DPS the front down
enemyKits.playWith: heavy peel — she's immobile and pure DPS
enemyKits.playAgainst: she has zero escape — hard-engage her and she dies first

enemyKits.combo: Smash 'n Grab pull → Crush knockup → Warlord's Challenge stun → bleed-stacked basics
enemyKits.winCondition: land the pull→knockup chain to lock a target while bleed and AS shred them
enemyKits.passive: basics stack a bleed for sustained damage
enemyKits.playWith: follow-up burst inside his knockup/stun window
enemyKits.playAgainst: telegraphed engage — bait Crush/Pull, then punish; kite his slow start

itemTimelineNote: estMin = first minute the role's measured median gold curve covers the cumulative inventory cost (slot order ≈ purchase order) — THEORY, no purchase timestamps in either API

fights.note: caught-out = deaths outside every clustered fight; costs/conversion use a 90s window over timeline majors (older film — no tower times; Fang/Prime/Orb only); itemGap counts participants' items est. online (median-gold model, THEORY)
